rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allylsulfonamides using CF3SO2Na as the CF3 radical source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) to generate CF3 radical via loss of SO2; the CF3 radical then adds to the allyl group of the N-allylsulfonamide

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from CF3 addition to allyl group

electron_flow: At the anode, CF3SO2Na is oxidized with loss of SO2 to give a CF3 radical, which adds to the alkene of the N-allylsulfonamide to form a carbon radical that undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the sulfonamide-tethered arene/heteroarene to give the cyclized product after rearomatization.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (no desired product) and BHT-CF3 adduct 6 detected by GC-MS, supporting a CF3 radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: crossover substrates (methyl 5-(N-acetyl-N-allylsulfamoyl)thiophene-2-carboxylate and N-allyl-N-((5-chlorothiophen-2-yl)sulfonyl)-4-oxopentanamide)
probe_reagents.observation: Products 3i (68%) and 3z4 (62%) were obtained with no crossover products, indicating the cyclization is intramolecular

notes: Mechanism inferred from the two control experiments shown (BHT radical quench and crossover). The intramolecular nature of the C-radical cyclization onto the (hetero)arene is supported by the absence of crossover products.
